steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence opens with an appositive phrase describing a medal recipient. The appositive must be immediately followed by the noun it names — that noun must be a person.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — starts with a non-person noun (results): Appositives must rename a person here; "results" cannot be a medal recipient. ✗
  • Choice B — starts with a non-person noun (introductions): Same violation. ✗
  • Choice C — starts with a non-person noun (limits): Same violation. ✗
  • Choice D — 'chemist Ahmed Zewail': A person immediately follows the appositive, satisfying the grammatical requirement. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
